{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Manager",
  "description": "Clinix Healthcare has 40 years of qualitative experience in Medical and Diagnostic services with over 120 centers across Africa. The state-of-the art Diagnostic Centre represents a milestone towards fulfilling Clinix strategic objectives in providing a world class integrated health system that ensures excellence in health and healthcare for all as a globally recognized destination for healthcare. Clinix Healthcare Nigeria is the first investment in West Africa. The Principal investor is Greenlife Pharmaceuticals and Bliss Gvs Pharmaceuticals. Both companies have outstanding repute in the Nigerian Healthcare industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Customer Service Manager

Job Location: Festac, Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

Job&nbsp;Description


	We are seeking an experienced, professional, and leadership-driven Customer Service Manager to oversee our customer service operations at the Badagry branch.
	The ideal candidate will ensure exceptional patient experience, efficient front-line service delivery, and continuous improvement of customer satisfaction.


Responsibilities


	Lead and supervise the customer service team, including receptionists, call center representatives, and front desk officers.
	Develop and implement strategies to enhance patient experience and service quality.
	Handle escalated customer complaints and complex enquiries professionally and promptly.
	Monitor daily operations at the front desk, appointment scheduling, and patient flow.
	Train, mentor, and evaluate customer service staff to maintain high performance standards.
	Analyze customer feedback and implement improvements to service processes.
	Ensure accurate documentation, billing support, and proper use of the hospital management system.
	Collaborate with clinical, laboratory, and administrative teams to ensure seamless patient care.
	Maintain high standards of professionalism, confidentiality, and patient data protection.
	Prepare daily/weekly reports on customer service performance and patient satisfaction.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Mass Communication, or any related field.
	4&ndash;6 years of customer service experience, with at least 2 years in a managerial or supervisory role.
	Previous experience in a hospital or healthcare environment is highly desirable.
	National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) certificate (completed).


Requirements &amp; Skills:


	Strong leadership and team management skills.
	Excellent communication, problem-solving, and conflict resolution abilities.
	Customer-focused mindset with a passion for delivering outstanding service.
	Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities effectively.
	Proficiency in hospital management systems, CRM software, and Microsoft Office tools.
	High level of integrity, empathy, and professionalism.
	Experience in the healthcare sector is a strong advantage.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;hr.clinixfestac@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-14",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Clinix Healthcare"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",

      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

    "name": "",
    "value": "197424"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",

    "value": {

      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,

    }

Customer Service Manager Job at Clinix Healthcare




        
    

        
        Clinix Healthcare has 40 years of qualitative experience in Medical and Diagnostic services with over 120 centers across Africa. The state-of-the art Diagnostic Centre represents a milestone towards fulfilling Clinix strategic objectives in providing a world class integrated health system that ensures excellence in health and healthcare for all as a globally recognized destination for healthcare. Clinix Healthcare Nigeria is the first investment in West Africa. The Principal investor is Greenlife Pharmaceuticals and Bliss Gvs Pharmaceuticals. Both companies have outstanding repute in the Nigerian Healthcare industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Manager
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Festac, Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking an experienced, professional, and leadership-driven Customer Service Manager to oversee our customer service operations at the Badagry branch.
	The ideal candidate will ensure exceptional patient experience, efficient front-line service delivery, and continuous improvement of customer satisfaction.


Responsibilities


	Lead and supervise the customer service team, including receptionists, call center representatives, and front desk officers.
	Develop and implement strategies to enhance patient experience and service quality.
	Handle escalated customer complaints and complex enquiries professionally and promptly.
	Monitor daily operations at the front desk, appointment scheduling, and patient flow.
	Train, mentor, and evaluate customer service staff to maintain high performance standards.
	Analyze customer feedback and implement improvements to service processes.
	Ensure accurate documentation, billing support, and proper use of the hospital management system.
	Collaborate with clinical, laboratory, and administrative teams to ensure seamless patient care.
	Maintain high standards of professionalism, confidentiality, and patient data protection.
	Prepare daily/weekly reports on customer service performance and patient satisfaction.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Mass Communication, or any related field.
	4–6 years of customer service experience, with at least 2 years in a managerial or supervisory role.
	Previous experience in a hospital or healthcare environment is highly desirable.
	National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) certificate (completed).


Requirements & Skills:


	Strong leadership and team management skills.
	Excellent communication, problem-solving, and conflict resolution abilities.
	Customer-focused mindset with a passion for delivering outstanding service.
	Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities effectively.
	Proficiency in hospital management systems, CRM software, and Microsoft Office tools.
	High level of integrity, empathy, and professionalism.
	Experience in the healthcare sector is a strong advantage.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sonographer",
  "description": "Clinix Healthcare has 40 years of qualitative experience in Medical and Diagnostic services with over 120 centers across Africa. The state-of-the art Diagnostic Centre represents a milestone towards fulfilling Clinix strategic objectives in providing a world class integrated health system that ensures excellence in health and healthcare for all as a globally recognized destination for healthcare. Clinix Healthcare Nigeria is the first investment in West Africa. The Principal investor is Greenlife Pharmaceuticals and Bliss Gvs Pharmaceuticals. Both companies have outstanding repute in the Nigerian Healthcare industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Sonographer

Job Location: Badagry, Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a skilled, experienced, and patient-focused Sonographer to join our radiology team at the Badagry branch.
	We are committed to delivering high-quality ultrasound diagnostic services with accuracy, safety, and compassion.


Responsibilities


	Perform high-quality ultrasound scans including abdominal, pelvic, obstetric, gynecological, vascular, and other specialized scans as required.
	Operate and maintain ultrasound equipment in accordance with manufacturer guidelines and safety standards.
	Ensure proper patient positioning, preparation, and comfort during procedures while explaining the process clearly.
	Produce accurate, high-resolution images and provide preliminary reports for review by radiologists or physicians.
	Collaborate with radiologists, obstetricians, and other clinicians to support timely and precise diagnosis.
	Maintain detailed patient records, scan logs, and documentation in line with regulatory requirements.
	Adhere strictly to infection control, patient confidentiality, and ultrasound safety protocols.
	Participate in quality assurance programs and equipment maintenance.
	Assist in training junior staff when necessary.


Qualifications


	Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Radiography / Sonography or Postgraduate Diploma in Medical Ultrasound from a recognized institution.
	Valid practicing license from the Radiographers Registration Board of Nigeria (RRBN).
	National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) certificate (completed).
	Minimum of 2&ndash;3 years of post-qualification experience in diagnostic ultrasound.
	Experience in a multi-specialty hospital or diagnostic center is an added advantage.


Requirements &amp; Skills:


	Valid practicing license from the Radiographers Registration Board of Nigeria (RRBN).
	Strong technical proficiency in ultrasound imaging and equipment operation.
	Excellent attention to detail and ability to interpret ultrasound images accurately.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills with a caring, patient-centered approach.
	Ability to work efficiently in a fast-paced environment while maintaining professionalism.
	Computer literate with proficiency in ultrasound reporting systems and PACS.
	Commitment to ethical practice, continuous professional development, and patient safety.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;hr.clinixfestac@gmail.com&nbsp;using the job title as the subject of the mail.",

Sonographer Job at Clinix Healthcare




        
    

        
        Clinix Healthcare has 40 years of qualitative experience in Medical and Diagnostic services with over 120 centers across Africa. The state-of-the art Diagnostic Centre represents a milestone towards fulfilling Clinix strategic objectives in providing a world class integrated health system that ensures excellence in health and healthcare for all as a globally recognized destination for healthcare. Clinix Healthcare Nigeria is the first investment in West Africa. The Principal investor is Greenlife Pharmaceuticals and Bliss Gvs Pharmaceuticals. Both companies have outstanding repute in the Nigerian Healthcare industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sonographer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Badagry, Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a skilled, experienced, and patient-focused Sonographer to join our radiology team at the Badagry branch.
	We are committed to delivering high-quality ultrasound diagnostic services with accuracy, safety, and compassion.


Responsibilities


	Perform high-quality ultrasound scans including abdominal, pelvic, obstetric, gynecological, vascular, and other specialized scans as required.
	Operate and maintain ultrasound equipment in accordance with manufacturer guidelines and safety standards.
	Ensure proper patient positioning, preparation, and comfort during procedures while explaining the process clearly.
	Produce accurate, high-resolution images and provide preliminary reports for review by radiologists or physicians.
	Collaborate with radiologists, obstetricians, and other clinicians to support timely and precise diagnosis.
	Maintain detailed patient records, scan logs, and documentation in line with regulatory requirements.
	Adhere strictly to infection control, patient confidentiality, and ultrasound safety protocols.
	Participate in quality assurance programs and equipment maintenance.
	Assist in training junior staff when necessary.


Qualifications


	Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Radiography / Sonography or Postgraduate Diploma in Medical Ultrasound from a recognized institution.
	Valid practicing license from the Radiographers Registration Board of Nigeria (RRBN).
	National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) certificate (completed).
	Minimum of 2–3 years of post-qualification experience in diagnostic ultrasound.
	Experience in a multi-specialty hospital or diagnostic center is an added advantage.


Requirements & Skills:


	Valid practicing license from the Radiographers Registration Board of Nigeria (RRBN).
	Strong technical proficiency in ultrasound imaging and equipment operation.
	Excellent attention to detail and ability to interpret ultrasound images accurately.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills with a caring, patient-centered approach.
	Ability to work efficiently in a fast-paced environment while maintaining professionalism.
	Computer literate with proficiency in ultrasound reporting systems and PACS.
	Commitment to ethical practice, continuous professional development, and patient safety.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the job title as the subject of the mail.

Senior Sales Executive Job at Shalina Healthcare




        
    

        
        Shalina Healthcare is one of the largest pharmaceutical companies in sub-Saharan Africa. For the last 35 years, we have made it our mission to provide quality medicines to those who need it the most. We manufacture and distribute branded prescription and over-the-counter pharmaceutical and consumer products across various therapeutic groups, including anti-malarial, antibiotics, anti-inflammatory, and nutrition. We have a track record few can match and our brands are among the most trusted in Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below: 

Job Position: Senior Sales Executive

Job ID: JOB-574
Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Department: Sales
Job type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Description


	Develop and execute sales strategies to achieve revenue targets and market share growth.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities with hospitals, clinics, and diagnostic centers.
	Lead, mentor, and motivate the sales team to achieve individual and team targets.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with healthcare professionals and key decision-makers.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and customer needs to adjust strategies.
	Prepare regular sales reports, forecasts, and performance reviews for management.
	Collaborate with marketing and operations teams to optimize service delivery and brand visibility.


Qualifications


	Candidates should possess relevant qualification with 3 - 5 Years of experience

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Officer",
  "description": "Natafamdavid Consulting Nigeria Limited is a business growth consultancy company offering tailor made services targeted at building profitable and productive companies with a focus on building sustainable enterprises, comprehensive HR advisory services, brand growth, corporate governance, business strategy and advisory services at different sections of the value chain.

Our services are across-the-board and we are driven by a strong passion to create value for our clients. From the het go, we set out to work with clients across diverse industries, providing strategic solutions to increase our success matrix, productivity, profitability.

So far, we have worked with clients in different industries and sectors such as Agriculture, Real Estate, Hospitality, Engineering &amp; Construction, Education, Logistics, Health &amp; Environment, Oil &amp; gas, Retail and more, to achieve their itemized objectives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Prepare and maintain accurate financial records and reports
	Manage accounts payable and receivable
	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
	Reconcile bank statements and ledger accounts
	Monitor cash flow, budgets, and expenditure
	Process payroll and statutory remittances
	Prepare tax schedules and ensure timely filing of taxes
	Assist in budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning
	Maintain proper documentation of invoices, receipts, and vouchers
	Support internal and external audit processes
	Ensure compliance with financial policies and regulatory standards


Requirements


	Minimum of HND / B.Sc. in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related field
	2&ndash;3 years of relevant work experience
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial reporting
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software
	High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills.


Preferred Skills:


	Knowledge of tax laws and statutory compliance
	Excellent communication and reporting skills
	Ability to work with minimal supervision
	Strong organizational and time-management abilities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;nadrecruitmentng@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  },

Account Officer Job at Natafamdavid Consulting Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Natafamdavid Consulting Nigeria Limited is a business growth consultancy company offering tailor made services targeted at building profitable and productive companies with a focus on building sustainable enterprises, comprehensive HR advisory services, brand growth, corporate governance, business strategy and advisory services at different sections of the value chain.

Our services are across-the-board and we are driven by a strong passion to create value for our clients. From the het go, we set out to work with clients across diverse industries, providing strategic solutions to increase our success matrix, productivity, profitability.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



So far, we have worked with clients in different industries and sectors such as Agriculture, Real Estate, Hospitality, Engineering & Construction, Education, Logistics, Health & Environment, Oil & gas, Retail and more, to achieve their itemized objectives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Prepare and maintain accurate financial records and reports
	Manage accounts payable and receivable
	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
	Reconcile bank statements and ledger accounts
	Monitor cash flow, budgets, and expenditure
	Process payroll and statutory remittances
	Prepare tax schedules and ensure timely filing of taxes
	Assist in budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning
	Maintain proper documentation of invoices, receipts, and vouchers
	Support internal and external audit processes
	Ensure compliance with financial policies and regulatory standards


Requirements


	Minimum of HND / B.Sc. in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related field
	2–3 years of relevant work experience
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial reporting
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software
	High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills.


Preferred Skills:


	Knowledge of tax laws and statutory compliance
	Excellent communication and reporting skills
	Ability to work with minimal supervision
	Strong organizational and time-management abilities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Technical Assistant Engineeer",
  "description": "Abelinis is a full-service brand experience agency, specializing in event and experiential marketing. We unite brands with their target audiences through live experiences, digital engagement and traditional marketing. With strategic insight, inspired creative, and flawless execution, Abelinis tells your story and brings your brand message to life.

We provide a comprehensive end to end event management service that spans a range of services, from venue sourcing to onsite presence and post event reporting. We can provide as little or as much support as you require, whether you&rsquo;re putting on a small CXO dinner or a huge annual conference.

At Abelinis we also ensure that your event is delivered to the highest possible standard with a bit of added &lsquo;pizzazz&rsquo;. Whether you are looking for an elaborate stage set, some lighting to set the mood or a theme to bind your event together or filming and live streaming; we can supply the creativity, equipment and manpower to produce everything from gala dinners and parties to memorable award ceremonies. Working with large, established international suppliers as well as smaller, regional audio visual companies, we have developed, and continue to develop, excellent relationships within the production and event equipment sector.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Technical Assistant Engineeer

Job Location:&nbsp;Akwa Ibom&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and technically sound Technical Assistant Engineer to support plant operations, maintenance activities, and construction/building activities in Akwaibom.
	The ideal candidate will assist in ensuring optimal equipment performance, compliance with safety standards, and continuous improvement of production efficiency.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide technical support to engineering and maintenance teams on-site
	Assist in monitoring plant equipment performance and troubleshooting faults
	Support preventive and corrective maintenance activities to ensure operational efficiency
	Prepare technical reports, logs, and documentation for engineering activities
	Ensure compliance with Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) standards
	Support process optimization and continuous improvement initiatives
	Liaise with cross-functional teams to resolve technical issues
	Assist in implementing engineering projects and upgrades
	Monitor spare parts usage and support inventory planning


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	B.Eng / HND in Mechanical, Building, Civil, or related Engineering field
	1&ndash;3 years of experience in a technical/engineering role (cement or manufacturing industry is an advantage)
	Basic knowledge of cement production processes and plant operations
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
	Proficiency in MS Office tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
	Good communication and teamwork skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced building and construction site.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@abelinisng.com&nbsp;using &quot;Technical Assistant Engineer &ndash; Akwa Ibom&quot;&nbsp; as the subject of the email.",

Technical Assistant Engineeer Job at Abelinis




        
    

        
        Abelinis is a full-service brand experience agency, specializing in event and experiential marketing. We unite brands with their target audiences through live experiences, digital engagement and traditional marketing. With strategic insight, inspired creative, and flawless execution, Abelinis tells your story and brings your brand message to life.

We provide a comprehensive end to end event management service that spans a range of services, from venue sourcing to onsite presence and post event reporting. We can provide as little or as much support as you require, whether you’re putting on a small CXO dinner or a huge annual conference.

At Abelinis we also ensure that your event is delivered to the highest possible standard with a bit of added ‘pizzazz’. Whether you are looking for an elaborate stage set, some lighting to set the mood or a theme to bind your event together or filming and live streaming; we can supply the creativity, equipment and manpower to produce everything from gala dinners and parties to memorable award ceremonies. Working with large, established international suppliers as well as smaller, regional audio visual companies, we have developed, and continue to develop, excellent relationships within the production and event equipment sector.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Technical Assistant Engineeer

Job Location: Akwa Ibom 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and technically sound Technical Assistant Engineer to support plant operations, maintenance activities, and construction/building activities in Akwaibom.
	The ideal candidate will assist in ensuring optimal equipment performance, compliance with safety standards, and continuous improvement of production efficiency.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide technical support to engineering and maintenance teams on-site
	Assist in monitoring plant equipment performance and troubleshooting faults
	Support preventive and corrective maintenance activities to ensure operational efficiency
	Prepare technical reports, logs, and documentation for engineering activities
	Ensure compliance with Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) standards
	Support process optimization and continuous improvement initiatives
	Liaise with cross-functional teams to resolve technical issues
	Assist in implementing engineering projects and upgrades
	Monitor spare parts usage and support inventory planning


Requirements & Qualifications


	B.Eng / HND in Mechanical, Building, Civil, or related Engineering field
	1–3 years of experience in a technical/engineering role (cement or manufacturing industry is an advantage)
	Basic knowledge of cement production processes and plant operations
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
	Proficiency in MS Office tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
	Good communication and teamwork skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced building and construction site.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Technical Assistant Engineer – Akwa Ibom"  as the subject of the email.
